channel.title: Compassion Benchmark — Daily Research
channel.description: Daily evidence-linked findings on how institutions recognize, respond to, and reduce suffering — scored across 1,155 entities.

item.title: Jul 1: China 19.5 of 100: Ethnic Unity and Progress Law in Force July 1 — Article 63 Diaspora Liability Active; Hong Kong's Last Pro-Democracy Party Dissolved
item.description: China's Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1. The law codifies existing assimilation policy, activates Article 63 extraterritorial criminal liability for diaspora members, and coincides with the dissolution of Hong Kong's last pro-democracy party. Both Amnesty International and Human R…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 30: DRC 2.3 of 100: Ebola Spread to a Fourth Province June 30 — 1,307 Cases, 377 Deaths; Haut-Uele Now Borders South Sudan at the Absolute Floor
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo's Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak spread to Haut-Uele province on June 30 — a fourth province affected — raising confirmed cases to 1,307 and deaths to 377. Haut-Uele borders South Sudan and the Central African Republic. South Sudan sits at the absolute floor at 0 o…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 29: Kuwait 25.0 and Bahrain 9.4 of 100: Iran Struck Both June 28 — Scores Unchanged; Harm Is Iran's Conduct, Not the Targets'
item.description: Iran fired missiles and drones at the US military base in Kuwait and the US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain on June 28. Kuwait intercepted two ballistic missiles — no deaths, two minor shrapnel injuries. A drone strike in Muharraq, Bahrain damaged a home — no casualties. Neither government's sco…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 28: Venezuela 18.0 of 100: Twin Earthquakes Killed 1,430-Plus — Government Accepted Aid, But Relief Was Slow and Opaque
item.description: June 28 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 15 assessments, zero score changes, and 12 false-alarm signals stopped — a new single-night record. The lead story is Venezuela. Twin magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes struck on June 24, killing more than 1,430 people and leaving about 68,900 families report…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 27: Lebanon 17.2 of 100: Framework Signed June 26, Rejected by Hezbollah Within Hours — War Continues
item.description: June 27 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 15 assessments, zero score changes, and 11 false-alarm signals stopped — a new single-night record, eclipsing the eight from the night before. The lead story is a diplomatic agreement that did not hold. Secretary Rubio announced an Israel-Lebanon framework o…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 26: Israel 0 of 100: UN Inquiry Finds Genocide Against Palestinian Children — About 20,000 Killed
item.description: June 26 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 15 assessments, zero score changes, and eight false-alarm signals stopped — doubling the previous single-night record set June 25. The cycle's lead finding is a landmark UN document. On June 23, the UN Commission of Inquiry (report A/HRC/62/CRP.2) found that…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 25: AI and Health Companies Face a Wave of Legal Actions — Scores Hold Until Courts Decide
item.description: June 25 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments, zero score changes, and four false-alarm signals stopped before they could distort the record. The cycle's defining theme is a wave of legal and regulatory actions against AI and health companies — none of which has yet produced a concluded p…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 24: Yemen 0 of 100: Houthi Authorities Detained 69 or More UN Staff — One Died in Custody
item.description: June 24 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments, zero score changes, and three false-alarm signals caught and discarded before they could distort the record. The most significant new evidence is from Yemen, where Houthi authorities arbitrarily detained at least 69 United Nations staff membe…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 23: Microsoft 65.3 Positive Watch: Covington Investigation Confirms MoD Azure Surveillance — Subscription Terminated, Five Governance Measures Enacted
item.description: June 23 is a zero-applied, confirmation-dominant cycle: 19 assessments across 3 indexes, 0 score changes, 19 confirmations, and 8 floor or near-floor reinforcements. The defining analytical feature is the inversion of the usual floor-cluster narrative: the highest-salience finding is a positive gove…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 22: South Sudan 0.0 Reinforced: 2018 Peace Deal Collapses toward Total War — UN Commission Documents Aerial Bombardment, 471,000 New IDPs
item.description: June 22 is a zero-applied, confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments across 3 indexes, 0 score changes, 20 confirmations, and 9 floor or near-floor reinforcements. The defining feature is the simultaneous depth of adverse evidence across four independent geographic crises: Sudan, South Sudan, DRC,…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 21: Humana Band Crossing Applied: 40.6 Functional → 35.2 Developing on DOJ Disability-Discrimination Complaint and AI Denial Class Action
item.description: June 21 is a single-downgrade cycle across 20 assessments in 3 indexes, producing 1 applied band crossing (Humana, Functional → Developing) and 19 confirmations with 7 floor/near-floor reinforcements. Humana's score moves to 35.2 Developing (from 40.6 Functional, -5.4, rank 180 → 305), applied today…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 20: University Index First Nightly Cycle: 10 Universities Assessed — All Hold via Victim/Perpetrator Attribution
item.description: June 20 is a zero-proposal cycle across 21 assessments in 3 indexes, producing 21 confirmations and 6 floor reinforcements. The analytically dominant event is the University Index's first nightly scan: 10 universities assessed as priority refinements, all holding at published composites. The methodo…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 19: Nigeria 21.9 → 18.0 (Δ -3.9): IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe for ~15,000 Borno Residents — Band Crossing Developing → Critical on Sub-Threshold Delta
item.description: Two formal score change proposals tonight: Figure AI moves from 37.5 to 31.3 (Δ -6.2) in the AI labs index, remaining in the Developing band; and Nigeria moves from 21.9 to 18.0 (Δ -3.9) in the countries index, crossing the Developing-to-Critical band boundary under the band-crossing rule — which ap…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 18: Humana 51.6 → 40.6 (Δ -11.0): HHS OIG Report Documents 72% LTCH and 54% IRF Medicare Advantage Denial Rates — 95% of Appeals Overturned
item.description: One formal score change proposal tonight: Humana moves from 51.6 to 40.6 (Δ -11.0) in the Fortune 500 index, remaining in the Functional band at its lower boundary. The trigger is the HHS Office of Inspector General report (surfaced June 12-15, 2026) documenting systematic inverse-prioritization of…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 17: Meta Platforms 7.8 Confirmed: K.G.M. Jury Verdict — First Adjudicated Social-Media-Addiction Liability Finding in MDL-3047
item.description: All 19 entities assessed tonight confirm at their published composites; zero change proposals were generated. The analytically dominant finding is the K.G.M. v. Meta Platforms and YouTube jury verdict (Los Angeles Superior Court, March 25, 2026): a jury found Meta and Google liable for platform-desi…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 16: Bangladesh 34.4 → 39.8 (Δ +5.4): ITUC 10-Worst Exit — First Since 2017 — November 2025 Labour Reforms Verified
item.description: One formal upgrade proposal tonight: Bangladesh moves from 34.4 to a proposed 39.8 (Δ +5.4, Developing band sustained) on the strength of its November 2025 labour law reforms — eased union formation thresholds, 120-day maternity leave, anti-discrimination protections, right to refuse unsafe work, ex…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 15: Oracle June 15 Severance-Forfeit Deadline Confirmed: Procedural Culmination of the Event That Set 14.7
item.description: Twenty-one entities assessed; zero change proposals generated. The benchmark's evidence methodology held across a day dominated by procedural culminations, pre-adjudication allegations, and ongoing humanitarian crises already priced into published scores. Oracle's June 15 sign-or-forfeit severance d…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 14: Bolivia 12.8 → Proposed 6.3 (Δ -6.5): June 9 State-of-Exception Law Confirms Predicted ACC-Floor Trigger
item.description: Tonight's cycle produced one change proposal and nineteen confirmations across 20 assessed entities. Bolivia is the lead signal: President Paz signed the Law Regulating States of Exception on June 9, 2026, authorizing military deployment against anti-government protesters, eliminating the prior 60-d…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 13: Anthropic 59.1 (Confirmed): First Government-Mandated AI Model Shutdown — Export-Control Compliance Scored as Transparent Conduct
item.description: Twenty entities assessed, zero change proposals generated. This is a confirmation-dominant cycle with substantive new evidence across five analytical threads. The lead event is Anthropic's June 12 Fable 5/Mythos 5 export-control shutdown — the first government-mandated suspension of commercial AI mo…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 12: Procter & Gamble 86.1 → 79.0 (Δ -7.1): BAND CROSSING Exemplary → Established — 7,000 Layoffs Despite Rising Profits
item.description: The June 12 cycle produces three scored change proposals — the highest single-cycle proposal count in the Fortune 500 index since the May methodology wave. Procter & Gamble (86.1 → 79.0, Δ -7.1) falls from Exemplary to Established: the 7,000-job restructuring announced despite increasing profits bre…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 11: Oracle Corporation 20.6 — WARN Deadline Passed; ~30,000 Terminations Executed; WA/MO Investigations Closed Without Ruling; Hold Maintained
item.description: Tonight's cycle assessed nineteen entities across four indexes — fourteen priority, five rotation — and confirmed every published score without generating a new scored proposal. Three domains dominate the analytical record. First, the Oracle WARN boundary ruling: the June 15 sign-or-forfeit deadline…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 10: Myanmar 0.0 — Bago Monastery Airstrike June 10: 28 Civilians Killed, Possible War Crime; Floor Reinforced
item.description: Tonight's cycle assessed twenty entities across four indexes — seventeen priority, three rotation — and confirmed every published score without generating a new scored proposal. The analytically significant work is concentrated in four domains. First, floor reinforcement: four entities at 0.0 absorb…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 9: UAE June 9 Colombian-Mercenary Evidence — Duplicate Suppressed; Open June 8 Band-Crossing Proposal Reinforced
item.description: Tonight's cycle assessed twenty entities across five indexes — fifteen priority, five rotation — and confirmed every published score without generating a new scored proposal. The analytically significant work is methodological: three rulings were resolved on record for the first time in this cycle s…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 8: United Arab Emirates 23.4 → 18.4 — ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY: Band Crossing Developing → Critical Proposed
item.description: One scored proposal this cycle — the first band crossing in several cycles. The UAE's published 23.4 priced it as an ordinary mid-band autocracy with constrained civic space and migrant-labor inequity. It did not price the documented, ongoing UAE financing, arming, and mercenary-supply of the RSF in…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 7: Microsoft 65.3 — COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION: Azure/Unit-8200 Remediation Earns Course-Correction Credit, Not Self-Initiation Premium; Positive-Watch Activated
item.description: Zero scored proposals this cycle — the pipeline's second no-proposal day in the current five-day sequence. The analytically consequential case is Microsoft: the June 8 announcement (Human Rights Review Board, mandatory pre-contract human rights impact assessments, termination of Unit 8200 Azure acce…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 6: Bolivia 28.4 → 18.4 (−10.0) — CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION: Signed Military-Force Law with Presumption-of-Legality Shield Crosses Developing to Critical
item.description: One change proposal this cycle. Bolivia (28.4→18.4, −10.0, Developing→Critical) is the only scored movement and a band crossing: President Paz signed a law (June 8, 2026) authorizing military force against protesters with a presumption-of-legality impunity shield — a codified structural state-conduc…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 5: Bolivia 28.4 → 22.8 (−5.6) — STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX: Lethal Force and Criminalization of Labor Leadership in Sixth Week of Protests
item.description: One change proposal this cycle. Bolivia (28.4→22.8, −5.6, remains Developing) is the only scored movement: six weeks of nationwide protests against the worst economic crisis in four decades met with lethal state force — 4 protesters killed, 127+ detained, and terrorism plus narco-funding charges fil…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 4: United States 23.4 → 17.5 (−5.9) — Band Crossing Proposed: Developing → Critical; ADJUDICATED-UNLAWFUL-CONDUCT-IS-SCORABLE
item.description: One change proposal this cycle, one new primary methodology ruling, and seven floor confirmations. The United States downgrade is the cycle's sole scored movement: federal court rulings by Judge Brian Murphy (D. Mass.) and Judge Jia Cobb (D.D.C.) found core fast-track and third-country deportation p…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 3: UnitedHealth Group 10.2 — Three Converging Sovereign Enforcement Actions; FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED Extended to Sovereign-Enforcement Density
item.description: No scored movements this cycle. The benchmark's analytical value tonight is concentrated in three sub-threshold findings that extend the scoring architecture in consequential directions. UnitedHealth Group's sovereign-enforcement density reaches a new threshold: the Massachusetts AG's $100M MassHeal…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 2: Germany Sub-Threshold: EU Return-Hubs Deal and Court-Ruled-Unlawful Border-Rejection Policy — New RETURN-HUBS-AND-NON-REFOULEMENT Ruling
item.description: No scored movements this cycle. The benchmark's intelligence value tonight is concentrated in sub-threshold evidence patterns that are pre-positioned as forward scoring triggers. Germany produces the cycle's most consequential new signal: the June 1 EU return-hubs framework, Afghanistan/Syria crimin…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark
